because.text: Accepted in full. F1 (Python/Go computed different WarrantIDs for U+0008/U+000C strings - Go stdlib escaping vs RFC 8785 short forms) was a silent split of the content-addressing primitive, fixed with an explicit JCS escaper in Go; F2 note-length bytes-vs-codepoints fixed with RuneCountInString; F3 binding-unverified MUST-report now emitted identically by both impls; F4 closed by tests/differential.py - 43 adversarial bodies asserted byte-identical across impls (incl. every control byte and the 200/201-char multibyte note boundary). Minors this commit: F5 Python enforces JCS-canonical ski blobs; F6 supersede subject MUST is now ERR in both impls; F7 Go verify-only scope documented as deliberate; F8 is_unverifiable aligned. Remaining accepted follow-ups: negative-path test suite (sigs, ski failures, supersede), keep sigma-conformance pinned as the cross-project invariant. First review of Warrant adjudicated inside Warrant's own store.

subject.note: Opus 4.8 adversarial review of Warrant v0.2 (4 MAJOR incl. silent WarrantID split; all fixed + verified)
